preamble: The diphthong [ɔɪ] reduces to schwa [ə] in unstressed syllables through stress-conditioned vowel reduction. This phonetic process reflects the natural simplification of diphthongs to the unmarked schwa vowel in weak prosodic positions.

examples.note: Words like 'boy' in unstressed contexts may reduce [ɔɪ] to schwa in rapid or casual speech.
